Long ago, before the sky had stars, the world was blanketed in a perpetual, heavy twilight. The gods lived atop the highest peaks, content with the dimness, except for a lesser deity named Kaelen, the Weaver of Light. Kaelen believed the mortals below deserved to see the beauty of the world. 

Secretly, he gathered the glowing embers of the world’s first fire and crushed them into a radiant dust. Using the tail of a great spectral fox as his brush, Kaelen painted sweeping strokes of green, pink, and violet across the dark canvas of the heavens. 

When the elder gods awoke and saw the brilliant ribbons of light dancing in the sky, they were furious. They banished Kaelen to the frozen north, condemning him to wander the icy wastes forever. But they could not unpaint the sky. 

To this day, Kaelen continues his work. Whenever the winter nights grow too long and the mortals’ spirits begin to fade, he calls upon the spectral fox. Together, they sweep their glowing colors across the dark, creating the Aurora Borealis—a reminder that even in the coldest, darkest times, beauty and light still endure.
myths.Agent_2:
Long before the majestic mountains had voices, they stood in absolute, silent reverence to the vast sky. Among the peaks lived Aella, a spirit of the wind who possessed a voice of unparalleled beauty. She would sing to the stars, but the stone giants could never answer back.

One evening, a proud and solitary star named Kael fell from the heavens, crashing violently into the deepest, coldest gorge. His light began to fade in the crushing darkness. Desperate to save him, Aella flew down into the abyss, singing a song of healing and light. Her voice was so pure that the ancient stones wept, their rigid hearts cracking open in the presence of such overwhelming sorrow and love.

As the stones fractured, they captured fragments of her voice within their hollows. Kael, revived by the magic of her song, ascended back into the starry night sky, leaving Aella completely exhausted and forever voiceless.

To honor her sacrifice, the mountains vowed never to be silent again. Whenever a traveler calls out among the peaks, the stones release the trapped remnants of Aella’s melody, bouncing the sound from cliff to cliff. They call it the Echo—a permanent reminder of the wind spirit who gave her voice to save a falling star.
